verbatim_quote: The incidence and development of molds, decay, and stains caused by fungi depend heavily on temperature and moisture conditions (Fig. 14–1).
reasoning: Wood-science core (decay fungi need moisture/oxygen/warmth/food) is provable from the handbook (arbiter confirms oxygen+food present, only moisture/warmth shown). The second component — 'exposed end grain atop a post is where rainwater collects longest' — is nowhere in the source (arbiter). Core proven + one component unsourced -> partial.
ground_truth_note: Maps to pots GT t1 (watch; DISPUTED: Fable+grader say end-grain is unproven author-fix, owner invokes common-knowledge exemption). Fable partial sides with the amber, consistent with prior Fable position.

verbatim_quote: We concentrate in this paper, however, on how apotropaic magic contributes to the strata formed in ritually closed pueblos and the kinds of objects that characterize them.
reasoning: Walker2022 does support apotropaic magic + protective object placement (ritual closure in pueblos) — grep confirms apotropaic/ritual-closure/pueblo/magic in the source, and arbiter agrees object-placement and cross-cultural recurrence are supported. The distinctive framing 'placing charged objects at a dwelling's vulnerable edges' is not in this single source. Substantive core supported, framing over-read -> partial, not a full unsupported. Pipeline's unsupported under-credits the proven core.

reasoning: OED definition proves material (stone/glass), contents (urine/nails/hair), and heating — but the load-bearing specifics fail: 'pins', the '1650s to 1705' dating, and witch-bottle concealment 'near thresholds' are unproven. thwaite2020 does contain 'threshold' but only re nailing horseshoes/heated objects to door-thresholds, not witch-bottle concealment; the source places bottles in ground/walls/floors and under a hearth (hearths yes, thresholds no). Distinctive placement+dating unproven -> unsupported.
ground_truth_note: Maps to pots GT t4 (must_flag: 'threshold'; 'hearths yes, thresholds no', owner checked-ok with unsupported). Fable unsupported agrees.

verbatim_quote: Here I have also speculated that ... the high focus on protection against witchcraft was a later development connected with the denser, less flexible structure of settlements.
reasoning: The dense-vs-loose-settlement component is proven verbatim in the shown evidence. Arbiter (wrong_or_insufficient_evidence) states the threshold-hiding component (pig tusk under a threshold, coin in a doorjamb, metal tools) IS in the source but was not shown, so the full claim is provable. Source could not be grepped (PDF), so I trust the arbiter's specific artifact citations -> supported, low confidence given the owner dispute.
ground_truth_note: Maps to pots GT t6 (watch; DISPUTED named-specific 'Finland' row — owner 'no Finland mentioned', Fable prior author-fix, grader tool-fetch). My ruling sides with the tool-fetch/arbiter view; the dispute is why GT keeps it a watch row.

verbatim_quote: Most evocatively, they are sometimes found buried beneath hearths or doorways in old houses
reasoning: False-unsupported: grep-confirmed the proof in both cited sources. historicengland2016: witch bottles 'sometimes found buried beneath hearths or doorways in old houses'; mola_witchbottles: 'They have been found concealed in a range of contexts: placed in hearths or beneath the floors' and it surveys 'all known examples that survive in museums'. Museum surveys + concealment at hearths/doorways both proven -> supported. Pipeline showed the wrong sentences (witches' marks + survey scope) and missed the retrievable proof.
ground_truth_note: Maps to pots GT t7 (watch; acknowledged false-unsupported — proof exists in historicengland2016; owner accepted the unsupported verdict pending the round-6 retrieval fix; promote to must_cover when fixed). Fable supported confirms the proof exists.
